'''Casey McGuire''' (born 24 January 1980) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]]  and up and coming [[New South Wales Rugby League|New South Wales]] debutante, he played his club football in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] (with whom he won the [[2006 NRL Premiership]]) and [[Parramatta Eels]], as well as in the [[Super League]] for the [[Catalans Dragons]].

==Early life==
Born 24 January 1980 in [[Nambour, Queensland]], McGuire was playing junior league for [[Caloundra Sharks]] & [[Kawana Dolphins]]. He then attended Caloundra State High QSSRL in 1997 when he was selected for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team]].

==Professional career==
===Parramatta Eels===
McGuire made his [[NRL]] debut in round 22 of the [[1998 NRL season|1998 season]] for the [[Parramatta Eels]] against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] at [[Penrith Park]].  The following week, he played in the club's 50–4 victory over [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] at [[Parramatta Stadium]].

===Brisbane Broncos===
The next season, he returned to Queensland to play for the [[Brisbane Broncos]]. At Brisbane, he was a utility player, normally used in games from the bench.  In the [[2002 NRL season]], he played in Brisbane's preliminary final defeat against eventual premiers the [[Sydney Roosters]].  McGuire subsequently played in the club's 2004 and 2005 finals campaigns.

McGuire played from the interchange bench in the [[2006 NRL Grand Final]] victory over [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] at [[Telstra Stadium]].

===Queensland===
He was selected for the [[Queensland Maroons]] in the [[2005 State of Origin series]] playing off the bench for games 1 and 2.
[[File:Casey McGuire.jpg|thumb|right|McGuire playing for Catalans in 2010]]

===Super League===
McGuire missed the Broncos' [[2007 World Club Challenge]] appearance due to his move to [[France]] to play in the [[Super League]] for [[Catalans Dragons]]. Having initially started playing {{rlp|so}} at Catalans Dragons, McGuire increasingly found himself playing at {{rlp|hk}} and sharing much of the creative responsibilities with [[Adam Mogg]] and [[Thomas Bosc]]. had also been named in the France squad for the [[Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] tournament, qualifying under the three-year residency rule, but did not play.<ref name="auto">{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailymercury.com.au/news/sacre-bleu/378994/|title = Sacre bleu! McGuire in French squad|newspaper = The Courier-Mail|date = 8 October 2009|last1 = Tuxworth|first1 = By Jon}}</ref>

===Return to Australia===
McGuire returned to Australia and the club where he made his NRL comeback with his first club, the [[Parramatta Eels]] for the [[2011 NRL season|2011 season]], playing as {{rlp|fe}}.  McGuire played 21 games in 2011 for Parramatta as the club narrowly avoided the wooden spoon.<ref name="auto1">{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-defeat-the-gold-coast-titans-to-avoid-wooden-spoon-for-2011/news-story/fc87216a6a23a4b8d0eefd6174b60c47/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat the Gold Coast Titans to avoid wooden spoon for 2011|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}}</ref>

A pectoral muscle injury during the [[2012 NRL season]] put the remainder of McGuire's playing career in doubt. McGuire returned for Parramatta in their final game of the season which came against [[St. George Illawarra]] in round 26 at [[ANZ Stadium]]. It was also the final game for retiring Parramatta players [[Nathan Hindmarsh]] and [[Luke Burt]].  Parramatta would go on to lose the match 29-8 and the club finished last for the first time since 1972.  The game was subsequently McGuire's last in the top grade.<ref name="auto3">{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2012/10/02/season-review-parramatta-eels/|title=Season review: Parramatta Eels|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2 October 2012}}</ref>
